section: Chapter 8: Nature of Matter – Elements, Compounds, and Mixtures

questions.question: What is a mixture?
questions.correct_answer: A combination of two or more substances where each retains its properties
questions.explanation: In mixtures, substances do not chemically react and can often be separated by physical methods.

questions.question: What are the components of a mixture?
questions.correct_answer: The individual substances that make up a mixture
questions.explanation: Each component in a mixture keeps its own physical and chemical properties.

questions.question: What is a uniform mixture?
questions.correct_answer: A mixture in which the components are evenly distributed
questions.explanation: In uniform mixtures, the different substances cannot be distinguished separately.

questions.question: What is a non-uniform mixture?
questions.correct_answer: A mixture where the components are not evenly distributed
questions.explanation: The components of such mixtures can usually be seen separately.

questions.question: Is air a mixture or a pure substance?
questions.correct_answer: Air is a mixture of gases
questions.explanation: Air contains nitrogen, oxygen, carbon dioxide, and other gases mixed uniformly.

questions.question: What is a pure substance in science?
questions.correct_answer: A substance made of only one type of particle
questions.explanation: Pure substances cannot be separated into other substances by physical processes.

questions.question: What are elements?
questions.correct_answer: Substances made of only one type of atom that cannot be broken down further
questions.explanation: Elements are the basic building blocks of matter.

questions.question: What is a compound?
questions.correct_answer: A substance formed when two or more elements combine chemically in a fixed ratio
questions.explanation: Compounds have properties different from the elements that form them.

questions.question: What compound is formed when iron and sulfur are heated together?
questions.explanation: Heating iron and sulfur causes them to chemically combine to form a new compound.

questions.question: What are minerals?
questions.correct_answer: Naturally occurring solid substances found in rocks
questions.explanation: Most minerals are compounds made of different elements.

notes.title: Mixtures
notes.content: Mixtures are formed when two or more substances combine without chemical reaction. Their components retain individual properties.

notes.title: Types of Mixtures
notes.content: Mixtures may be uniform (homogeneous) or non-uniform (heterogeneous) depending on how evenly components are distributed.

notes.title: Pure Substances
notes.content: Pure substances consist of only one type of particle and include elements and compounds.

notes.title: Elements
notes.content: Elements are the simplest substances that cannot be broken down into simpler substances by chemical reactions.

notes.title: Compounds
notes.content: Compounds are formed when elements combine chemically in fixed ratios and have different properties from their constituent elements.

facts.fact: Air is a uniform mixture mainly composed of nitrogen and oxygen.
There are currently 118 known chemical elements.
Water is a compound made of hydrogen and oxygen in a 2:1 ratio.
Alloys such as brass and bronze are mixtures of metals.
